title: evolvulus glomeratus subsp. grandiflorus
intro: Evolvulus glomeratus subsp. grandiflorus, also known as dwarf morning glory or blue myrtle, is a flowering plant native to tropical regions, characterized by its small, delicate blue flowers and small leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Evolvulus glomeratus subsp. grandiflorus include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
